title: DFU Flow: Device disconnected before tapping Continue on Software Update page — headset power off
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap on a connected headset device (e.g., Poly Voyager 5200)
Navigate to Settings → Software Update
Verify the Software Update page is displayed with device info and 'Continue' button
Press and hold the Call button on the headset for 5 seconds to power off the device
Wait 3 seconds
Tap the 'Continue' button on the Software Update page
Observe the app response
expected: The app MUST NOT proceed to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. Instead, the app should display an appropriate error message such as 'Device disconnected' or 'Connection lost' and block the DFU flow. The user should remain on the Software Update page or be returned to the device list.

title: DFU Flow: Device disconnected before tapping Continue on Software Update page — earbud placed in charging case
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap on a connected earbud device (e.g., Poly Voyager Free 20)
Navigate to Settings → Software Update
Verify the Software Update page is displayed with device info and 'Continue' button
Place both earbuds into the charging case and close the lid
Wait 5 seconds for the device to disconnect
Tap the 'Continue' button on the Software Update page
Observe the app response
expected: The app MUST NOT proceed to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. Instead, the app should display an appropriate error message such as 'Device disconnected' or 'Connection lost' and block the DFU flow. The user should remain on the Software Update page or be returned to the device list.

title: DFU Flow: Device disconnected before tapping Continue on Software Update page — Bluetooth turned off on phone
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap on a connected device (e.g., Poly Sync 40)
Navigate to Settings → Software Update
Verify the Software Update page is displayed with device info and 'Continue' button
Open phone Settings → Bluetooth → Toggle Bluetooth OFF
Return to Poly Studio Mobile app
Wait 3 seconds
Tap the 'Continue' button on the Software Update page
Observe the app response
expected: The app MUST NOT proceed to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. Instead, the app should display an appropriate error message such as 'Device disconnected' or 'Bluetooth turned off' and block the DFU flow. The user should remain on the Software Update page or be returned to the device list.

title: DFU Flow: Device disconnected before tapping Continue — then reconnected before tapping Continue
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap on a connected headset device (e.g., Poly Voyager 5200)
Navigate to Settings → Software Update
Verify the Software Update page is displayed with device info and 'Continue' button
Press and hold the Call button on the headset for 5 seconds to power off the device
Wait 3 seconds
Power on the headset by pressing and holding the Call button for 3 seconds
Wait for the device to reconnect (verify Bluetooth icon in app shows connected state)
Tap the 'Continue' button on the Software Update page
Observe the app response
expected: After reconnection, tapping 'Continue' should proceed normally to the 'Three Easy Steps' page, as the device is now connected. The app should correctly detect the reconnected state and allow the DFU flow to continue.

title: DFU Flow: Device disconnected during Software Update page — app backgrounded and restored
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap on a connected device (e.g., Poly Voyager 5200)
Navigate to Settings → Software Update
Verify the Software Update page is displayed with device info and 'Continue' button
Press the Home button to background the app
While app is backgrounded, press and hold the Call button on the headset for 5 seconds to power off the device
Wait 5 seconds
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app from the app switcher
Verify the current screen shown
Tap the 'Continue' button on the Software Update page
Observe the app response
expected: When the app is restored, it should detect the disconnected state. The 'Continue' button should either be disabled or tapping it should show an error message. The app MUST NOT proceed to the 'Three Easy Steps' page.

title: DFU Flow: Device disconnected during Software Update page — app force-killed and relaunched
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap on a connected device (e.g., Poly Voyager 5200)
Navigate to Settings → Software Update
Verify the Software Update page is displayed with device info and 'Continue' button
Press and hold the Call button on the headset for 5 seconds to power off the device
Wait 3 seconds
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe away from recent apps)
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app
Observe the initial screen shown after relaunch
If the device is still shown in the device list, tap on it and navigate to Settings → Software Update
Observe the Software Update page behavior
expected: After force-kill and relaunch, the app should show the device list. If the device is disconnected, it should show as 'Not Connected' or similar. Navigating to Software Update should either be blocked or show a 'Device not connected' message. The 'Three Easy Steps' page must NOT be shown for a disconnected device.

title: DFU Flow: Verify 'Three Easy Steps' page is never shown for disconnected device — multiple disconnection methods
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap on a connected device
Navigate to Settings → Software Update
Verify the Software Update page is displayed
Disconnect the device using one of the following methods: a) Power off the device, b) Place earbuds in charging case, c) Turn off phone Bluetooth, d) Move device out of Bluetooth range (>30 meters)
Wait 5 seconds
Tap the 'Continue' button
Verify the app does NOT navigate to the 'Three Easy Steps' page
If the app shows an error, dismiss it
Reconnect the device
Navigate back to Settings → Software Update
Repeat steps 4-8 for each disconnection method (a, b, c, d) listed in step 5
expected: For ALL disconnection methods, the app MUST NOT navigate to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. The app should consistently detect the disconnected state and block the DFU flow with an appropriate error message or disabled button.
